conversations.content: Today we studied the Pan-Asia StarLink Aerospace Manufacturing Plant Zero-G Assembly Bay Safety Code. If a sudden drop in the spacesuit oxygen supply reading is detected inside the bay...
Sudden drop in oxygen supply? The astronaut's life is in danger! What's the first step to rescue?
then switch to emergency high-pressure oxygen cylinder supply, then guide personnel to the nearest safe airlock.
Switch to emergency oxygen plus guide to airlock — life first! Any other space emergencies?

If micro-meteoroid penetration causing cabin depressurization and air leak is detected, then release polymer quick-setting foam to seal the breach, then automatically close the isolation valve of the damaged section.
Micro-meteoroid puncture? Quick-setting foam to seal plus close isolation valve — brilliant! What's the third scenario?
If entanglement or risk of breakage of an extravehicular activity tether is detected, then activate the emergency retrieval winch, then dispatch a manual propulsion unit for assistance.
Tether entanglement — retrieval winch plus manual propulsion unit assistance — foolproof! When any of these happens, what must ultimately be done?

If any of the above situations occurs, then the space station's red abandon-module drill alarm must be triggered, all extravehicular operations must be halted, and all personnel must be forced into the lifeboat to stand by.

think: Pan-Asia StarLink zero-g assembly bay: three anomalies: spacesuit oxygen drop → emergency high-pressure oxygen + guide to airlock; micro-meteoroid penetration depressurization → quick-setting foam seal + close isolation valve; tether entanglement/breakage risk → retrieval winch + manual propulsion unit. Any case → red abandon-module alarm + halt EVA operations + all personnel to lifeboat standby.
memory: Pan-Asia StarLink Aerospace Manufacturing Plant Zero-G Assembly Bay Safety Code: If a sudden drop in the spacesuit oxygen supply reading is detected inside the bay, then switch to emergency high-pressure oxygen cylinder supply, then guide personnel to the nearest safe airlock; if micro-meteoroid penetration causing cabin depressurization and air leak is detected, then release polymer quick-setting foam to seal the breach, then automatically close the isolation valve of the damaged section; if entanglement or risk of breakage of an extravehicular activity tether is detected, then activate the emergency retrieval winch, then dispatch a manual propulsion unit for assistance; if any of the above situations occurs, then the space station's red abandon-module drill alarm must be triggered, all extravehicular operations must be halted, and all personnel must be forced into the lifeboat to stand by.
